div.h3: W^X (Write XOR Execute) Security Model Enforcement

p.em: Memory Management Unit & OS Loader Logic

div.p: Implementation Note: Modern CPUs enforce this via the NX (No-Execute) or XD (Execute Disable) bit in the Page Table Entry (PTE). The OS kernel prevents mmap calls that violate the W^X policy unless explicitly overridden (e.g., legacy apps or specific JIT engines).
Transition Rule: Memory must be immutable before it can be marked as executable.
